title: fix runlabel functions based on QA feedback

comments.body:
Hi @baude 
Test with the patch. Seems -p will pull image no matter the image is exist or not. This is not consistent with the help text. 

And I have a question about the code. Seems we get opt from env but will make a replacement for image and name. With this implement, we should treat image, name differently with opt when we add labels to image. It is a little wired for me. Is it possible to read them all from env and keep image, name and opts with the same format in the label?

I just noticed a problem with optional arguments that are appended to the rulabel command e.g., `podman container runlabel LABEL IMAGE args`. `args` can make some troubles for CLI parsing as it will be interpreted as a CLI flag such at the moment (e.g., `args="-h"`).

> Hi @baude
> Test with the patch. Seems -p will pull image no matter the image is exist or not. This is not consistent with the help text.
> 
> And I have a question about the code. Seems we get opt from env but will make a replacement for image and name. With this implement, we should treat image, name differently with opt when we add labels to image. It is a little wired for me. Is it possible to read them all from env and keep image, name and opts with the same format in the label?

Fixed the logic of -p.  I think the code is behaving as expected? And consistent with atomic cli.  Do you disagree?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Fixed the logic of -p. I think the code is behaving as expected? And consistent with atomic cli. Do you disagree?

No, this is not consistent with atomic cli. In atomic cli the image and name is also work with "$".  Here is the details:

```
#  podman container runlabel  install rhel7/etcd:latest
Command: /proc/self/exe run --rm  --privileged -v /:/host -e HOST=/host -e NAME= -e IMAGE=   /usr/bin/install.sh  
unable to pull : error getting default registries to try: repository name must have at least one component
`/proc/self/exe run --rm  --privileged -v /:/host -e HOST=/host -e NAME= -e IMAGE=   /usr/bin/install.sh  ` failed: exit status 125
```

And here is the install label of the etcd image:
```
            "install": "/usr/bin/docker run --rm $OPT1 --privileged -v /:/host -e HOST=/host -e NAME=$NAME -e IMAGE=$IMAGE $IMAGE $OPT2 /usr/bin/install.sh  $OPT3",
```

> Valentin and I agreed he would submit a PR following mine given it is a change in behavior. I support that.

Here's the PR: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1637
